subject: Mathematics
title: Boolean Algebra and Circuits
description: A short lesson to help you apply Boolean Algebra and Circuits.

hook.text: Every phone, laptop, and smart device runs on a type of algebra invented in 1854.

buildup.text: George Boole created an algebra of logic using just two values: 0 and 1, with AND, OR, and NOT.

discovery.text: In the 1930s, Claude Shannon realized Boolean algebra perfectly describes electrical switch circuits.

twist.text: Shannon's insight built the bridge from abstract logic to physical computers—the most consequential link in tech history.

climax.text: Every digital device—from calculators to supercomputers—is built from Boolean logic gates.

punchline.text: An 1854 algebra of thought became the language of every computer.

quiz.question: Who showed that Boolean algebra models electrical circuits?
